- 96 ### 7. Safe autonomy
+ 96 ### 7. Repo-owned Pi resource strategy
+ 97 Because Pinchy uses Pi as its execution backend, Pinchy can reuse valuable Pi skills, prompts, and extensions where appropriate.
+ 98 
+ 99 However, Pinchy should prefer **repo-owned copies** over hidden machine-local dependencies.
+100 That means:
+101 - useful Pi skills may be imported into this repository
+102 - imported skills should be adapted to Pinchy’s product model and terminology
+103 - Pinchy should version and review those imported resources in-repo
+104 - the repository should remain runnable without relying on a developer’s private Pi config state
+105 
+106 ### 8. Safe autonomy
  97 When the user is away, Pinchy should be able to continue bounded work safely by scheduling autonomous runs and allowing Pi to execute those runs.
  98 
  99 ## Responsibility split
 100 
     ...
 213 - richer operator UI for conversations, runs, questions, artifacts, approvals, and health
 214 
 215 These clients should talk to Pinchy’s API and shell state.
 216 
+227 ### G. Repo-local Pi resources
+228 Pinchy should maintain a strong repo-local `.pi` layer.
+229 
+230 This includes:
+231 - repo-local skills
+232 - repo-local prompts
+233 - repo-local extensions
+234 - Pinchy-specific guidance layered on top of Pi capabilities
+235 
+236 When useful skills already exist in an external or broader Pi configuration, Pinchy may import them into this repository, but should:
+237 - review them before adoption
+238 - adapt them to Pinchy’s architecture and workflow language
+239 - keep them under version control here
+240 - avoid depending on personal machine-only Pi configuration as a hidden runtime requirement
+241 
 217 ## Mental model
 218 
 219 A useful mental model is:
 220 - **Pinchy = supervisor shell / persistent platform**
     ...

 452 
 453 The goal is to make the worker Pi-backed without leaking raw Pi runtime details throughout Pinchy.

details.title: Phase 4: Build Pi integration facade for Pinchy worker
details.prompt: Implement Phase 4 from docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md using TDD. Add a Pinchy-owned Pi integration facade that wraps @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent for run execution and later resume. Keep Pinchy responsible for orchestration/state, Pi responsible for within-run execution. Start with the smallest useful failing tests. Prefer small modules, explicit names, and minimal diffs. Reuse existing apps/host runtime patterns where possible. Validate with npm test and npm run check.

details.title: Phase 5: Audit and import high-value repo-local Pi skills
details.prompt: Execute Phase 5 from docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md. Audit useful Pi skills/prompts/extensions available for reuse, import the highest-value safe candidates into this repository's .pi layer, adapt them to Pinchy terminology and architecture, and keep them repo-owned. Avoid secrets or hidden machine-only dependencies. Use tests or focused validation where practical, and update docs if standard workflow changes.

details.title: Phase 6: Add Pinchy runtime config layer
details.prompt: Implement Phase 6 from docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md with TDD where practical. Add a Pinchy-owned runtime config layer for non-secret defaults such as provider, model, and thinking profile. Keep it separate from auth/session secrets and machine-private Pi state. Add focused tests and validate with npm test and npm run check.

details.title: Phase 7-10: Make worker fully Pi-backed with resume, dashboard integration, and autonomous QA scheduling
details.prompt: Continue executing docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md after the Pi facade and runtime config exist. Make services/agent-worker fully Pi-backed, add reply-driven run resume behavior, wire dashboard/backend surfaces to persistent conversations/runs/questions where practical, and implement autonomous QA/bugfix run scheduling around Pi-backed runs. Use TDD for behavior changes. Keep Pinchy as the shell/orchestrator and Pi as the execution substrate. Re-run validation after each bounded slice.

details.title: Phase 10-12: Add first notification adapter and continue toward full plan completion
details.prompt: Continue the remaining phases from docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md in bounded, test-driven slices. Prioritize the first async notification adapter (Discord), then additional channel scaffolding, then UX refinement. Keep changes local-first, auditable, and repo-scoped. Summarize progress and residual gaps in docs if full completion is not reached in one pass.

+25 ## Runtime defaults
+26 
+27 Pinchy can load non-secret runtime defaults from `.pinchy-runtime.json`.
+28 
+29 Example:
+30 
+31 ```json
+32 {
+33   "defaultProvider": "openai",
+34   "defaultModel": "gpt-5.4",
+35   "defaultThinkingLevel": "medium"
+36 }
+37 ```
+38 
+39 Environment overrides are also supported:
+40 - `PINCHY_DEFAULT_PROVIDER`
+41 - `PINCHY_DEFAULT_MODEL`
+42 - `PINCHY_DEFAULT_THINKING_LEVEL`
+43 
+44 These defaults are intended for portable runtime preferences only.
+45 Do not put secrets, auth tokens, or private session state in `.pinchy-runtime.json`.
+46

details.diff:
...
  69 - `npm run dashboard:web` — richer React/Vite dashboard app
  70 
  71 The React dashboard can now issue one-click runtime reload requests. When the Pinchy daemon is running, it consumes the reload request and triggers `/reload-runtime` directly inside the Pi session.
  72 
+ 73 ## Runtime services
+ 74 
+ 75 Current local entrypoints include:
+ 76 - `npm run agent` — interactive Pi-backed Pinchy shell
+ 77 - `npm run daemon` — recurring autonomous maintenance/debugging loop
+ 78 - `npm run api` — Pinchy control-plane API on port `4320`
+ 79 - `npm run worker` — Pi-backed Pinchy worker loop for queued and resumable runs
+ 80 - `npm run dashboard` — server-rendered dashboard + local API on port `4310`
+ 81 - `npm run dashboard:web` — React dashboard app on port `4311`
+ 82 
+ 83 Pinchy can also load non-secret runtime defaults from `.pinchy-runtime.json`, including:
+ 84 - `defaultProvider`
+ 85 - `defaultModel`
+ 86 - `defaultThinkingLevel`
+ 87 
+ 88 For the first async notification adapter, Discord webhook delivery is supported through:
+ 89 - `PINCHY_DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL`
+ 90 
  73 ## Run locally
  74 
  75 ```bash
  76 cd pinchy-dev
  77 npm install
  78 npm run agent
  79 npm run daemon
+ 98 npm run api
+ 99 npm run worker
  80 npm run dashboard
  81 npm run dashboard:web
  82 npm test
  83 ```

...
 43 
 44 These defaults are intended for portable runtime preferences only.
 45 Do not put secrets, auth tokens, or private session state in `.pinchy-runtime.json`.
 46 
+47 ## API mode
+48 
+49 ```bash
+50 npm run api
+51 ```
+52 
+53 Default port:
+54 - `4320` — Pinchy control-plane API
+55 
+56 ## Worker mode
+57 
+58 ```bash
+59 npm run worker
+60 ```
+61 
+62 This runs the Pi-backed Pinchy worker loop for queued runs and resumable waiting runs.
+63 
+64 ## Notification defaults
+65 
+66 The first async notification adapter currently supported is Discord via webhook:
+67 - `PINCHY_DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL`
+68 
+69 If this variable is not configured, Discord delivery attempts are recorded as failed delivery records instead of silently succeeding.
+70

+22 ## Browser debugging
+23 
+24 Useful tools:
+25 - `browser_debug_scan`
+26 - `browser_dom_snapshot`
+27 - `browser_run_probe`
+28 - `browser_execute_steps`
+29 - `browser_compare_artifacts`
+30 
+31 Suggested workflow:
+32 1. run `browser_debug_scan` first to collect screenshot, console issues, and failing requests
+33 2. use `browser_dom_snapshot` when you need saved HTML and visible-text evidence
+34 3. use `browser_run_probe` for quick selector/text checks
+35 4. use `browser_execute_steps` for bounded multi-step reproduction flows
+36 5. use `browser_compare_artifacts` to compare before/after screenshots or DOM snapshots
+37 
+38 This keeps website debugging evidence-first and aligned with the repo’s website investigation skills.
+39 
 22 ## OCR and screen targeting

 24 Useful tools:

+40 ## Local app debugging
+41 
+42 Useful tools:
+43 - `desktop_screenshot`
+44 - `active_app_info`
+45 - `desktop_ui_snapshot`
+46 - `desktop_open_app`
+47 
+48 Suggested workflow:
+49 1. capture a `desktop_screenshot` first for current app state
+50 2. inspect the frontmost app/window with `active_app_info`
+51 3. use `desktop_ui_snapshot` for lightweight control visibility before interacting
+52 4. only use `desktop_open_app` when clearly helpful and after approval
+53 
+54 This keeps local app debugging observation-first and aligned with the repo’s app-debugger workflow.
+55

details.title: Roadmap Task 1: Structured run outcomes and worker transitions
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 1. Implement Phase 1: structured run outcomes and worker transition handling. Use TDD. Upgrade the Pi facade and worker so runs can end as completed, waiting_for_human, waiting_for_approval, or failed. Add focused transition helpers instead of spreading state changes inline. Keep Pinchy as the orchestrator and Pi as the within-run executor. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 2: Blocked question delivery scheduling and delivery API visibility
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 2. Implement Phase 2 with TDD. Add blocked question delivery scheduling, persist notification attempts, and expose delivery visibility through the Pinchy API. Keep changes small and cohesive. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 3: Inbound reply normalization and resume-safe reply ingestion
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 3. Implement inbound reply normalization and make reply ingestion safe for run resume behavior. Use tests first. Reuse the existing Pinchy reply model and Pi-backed resume path. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 4: Convert daemon autonomous work into queued Pinchy runs
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 4. Refactor the daemon so autonomous goals, watcher follow-ups, and iteration-style autonomous work enqueue persistent Pinchy runs instead of directly prompting Pi for recurring autonomous execution. Keep the daemon as producer and worker as consumer. Use TDD for behavior changes and validate with npm test and npm run check.

details.title: Roadmap Task 5: First-class autonomous QA run kind and scheduling
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 5. Add explicit run kinds/intents and make autonomous QA a first-class Pinchy run kind processed through the worker and Pi facade. Use minimal schema and focused tests. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 6: Expand API into main control plane
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 6. Expand the Pinchy API with run detail, question detail, delivery listing, and run control endpoints that make the API the main backend surface for dashboard/app clients. Use TDD and validate with npm test and npm run check.

details.title: Roadmap Task 7: Wire dashboard to conversations runs questions and deliveries
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 7. Make the dashboard a real operator UI over persistent conversations, runs, questions, replies, and deliveries. Prefer small validated slices and reuse the API as the primary data source. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 8: Finish first end-to-end async notification channel
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 8. Finish one async messaging channel end-to-end, prioritizing Discord outbound plus inbound reply support, and scaffold other channels only if it stays bounded. Keep the work local-first and auditable. Re-run npm test and npm run check before stopping.

details.title: Roadmap Task 9: Operational hardening and observability
details.prompt: Follow docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md Task 9. Improve health visibility, audit clarity, run IDs in logs, failure summaries, and operational docs so overnight unattended use is observable and debuggable. Use focused tests where practical and validate with npm test and npm run check.

details.title: Roadmap bookkeeping: keep roadmap state updated while executing roadmap tasks
details.prompt: While executing docs/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md, keep docs/ROADMAP_STATUS.md and .pinchy-roadmap-state.json updated step by step. Mark only one roadmap task as in_progress at a time, mark tasks done only after validation passes, record residual gaps in notes when partial, and update lastUpdated/currentTask/currentPhase/overallState as work progresses.
